collections.title: Starter Pack
collections.description: Recommended first install for Bash framework maintenance with tests, linting, and performance checks.

skills.name: coverage-gap-planner
skills.description: Use when Bash maintenance work needs a deliberate plan for which untested behaviors to cover next, especially after failures or shared-module edits.
skills.author: unknown
skills.whyHere: Keeps the workflow shelf focused on high-yield Bash coverage gaps instead of broad low-value test expansion.

skills.name: bash-bats-loop
skills.description: Use when maintaining Bash code and the next move should be a fast Bats regression loop that narrows failures before editing more code.
skills.author: unknown
skills.whyHere: Anchors the workflow shelf with a concrete Bats-driven regression loop for Bash framework maintenance and fast confidence after edits.

skills.name: shellcheck-triage
skills.description: Use when Bash maintenance work should be driven by ShellCheck findings, especially when warnings need prioritization and safe fixes.
skills.author: unknown
skills.whyHere: Adds a lint-first Bash triage path so warning cleanup is prioritized by behavior risk rather than bulk suppression.

skills.name: hyperfine-benchmark-loop
skills.description: Use when Bash performance work should be measured with repeatable hyperfine benchmarks instead of intuition or one-off timing.
skills.author: unknown
skills.whyHere: Gives the backend shelf an evidence-based hyperfine loop for Bash runtime tuning and regression checks.

skills.name: bash-maintenance-orchestrator
skills.description: Use when a Bash maintenance task needs coordinated testing, linting, and benchmarking instead of treating those loops as separate afterthoughts.
skills.author: unknown
skills.whyHere: Provides the cross-loop execution order for Bash maintenance tasks where tests, linting, and performance checks must be coordinated deliberately.
